  the granted permissions to the minimum required.

---

# rubrik_azure_permission_groups (Data Source)

The `rubrik_azure_permission_groups` data source returns the permission groups
available for a single RSC Azure feature, along with the Azure RBAC actions
and data actions that each permission group requires. It exposes the same
catalog used by RSC itself, so configurations can discover the available
groups (for example, the `BASIC` and `RECOVERY` split on
`AZURE_SQL_DB_PROTECTION`) at plan time.

Each statement carries the scope it applies to and the kind of operation it
authorises. Azure RBAC distinguishes management-plane operations (`actions`)
from data-plane operations (`data_actions`), and many features require
permissions at both the subscription and resource group scopes.

The statements returned are informational. To get the permission set RSC
actually requires for a specific `(feature, permission_groups)` combination,
feed the discovered groups into the `rubrik_azure_permissions` data source —
it returns the policy-shaped `subscription_actions`,
`subscription_data_actions`, `resource_group_actions` and
`resource_group_data_actions` lists ready to drive an Azure role definition.

~> **Note:** RSC follows a least-privilege model: a permission group should be
opted into only when its capabilities are required. For example, `RECOVERY`
grants the elevated Azure permissions needed to perform recovery operations
and should be configured only on accounts that need to perform recoveries.
Hard-coding a known set of permission groups is a valid choice when it keeps
the granted permissions to the minimum required.

To look up multiple features at once, use `for_each` on the data source.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up the latest permission groups available for a single RSC Azure feature.
data "rubrik_azure_permission_groups" "cnp" {
  feature = "CLOUD_NATIVE_PROTECTION"
}

# Splat over permission_groups to get just the group names — feed straight
# into a rubrik_azure_subscription feature block instead of hard-coding them.
output "cnp_permission_groups" {
  value = data.rubrik_azure_permission_groups.cnp.permission_groups[*].name
}

# Chain into rubrik_azure_permissions to get the policy-shaped permission
# lists that drive an Azure role definition for this feature.
data "rubrik_azure_permissions" "cnp" {
  feature           = data.rubrik_azure_permission_groups.cnp.feature
  permission_groups = data.rubrik_azure_permission_groups.cnp.permission_groups[*].name
}

# Look up several features at once with for_each.
data "rubrik_azure_permission_groups" "all" {
  for_each = toset([
    "CLOUD_NATIVE_PROTECTION",
    "AZURE_SQL_DB_PROTECTION",
    "EXOCOMPUTE",
  ])

  feature = each.key
}

output "permission_groups_by_feature" {
  value = {
    for f, d in data.rubrik_azure_permission_groups.all :
    f => d.permission_groups[*].name
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `feature` (String) RSC feature name to look up permission groups for (e.g. `CLOUD_NATIVE_PROTECTION`).

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) SHA-256 hash of the permission groups and statements returned.
- `permission_groups` (Attributes Set) Permission groups available for the feature. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--permission_groups))

<a id="nestedatt--permission_groups"></a>
### Nested Schema for `permission_groups`

Read-Only:

- `name` (String) Permission group name.
- `statements` (Attributes Set) Azure RBAC permissions required by this permission group, one entry per `(scope, kind, permission, use_case)` tuple. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--permission_groups--statements))
- `version` (Number) Permission group version.

<a id="nestedatt--permission_groups--statements"></a>
### Nested Schema for `permission_groups.statements`

Read-Only:

- `kind` (String) Azure RBAC permission kind. One of `action` (management plane) or `data_action` (data plane).
- `permission` (String) Azure RBAC permission string (e.g. `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/read`).
- `scope` (String) Azure RBAC scope at which the permission applies. One of `subscription` or `resource_group`.
- `use_case` (String) Use case the permission is required for. May be empty.
